acceptance_criteria: Memo clearly maps rows to workbook cells and documents decision logic with formula references
CSV export allows a reviewer to validate numeric computations without openpyxl
Grader/verifier metadata no longer asserts semantic correctness without supporting cell-level evidence or includes a clear qualification
quality_checks: Verify computed_rows.csv values exactly match decision_summary in actual_outputs.metadata_json.original_fields.decision_summary
Confirm appendix cell addresses point to correct formula outputs in the XLSX
Run basic repro script to regenerate workbook and CSV in CI (if possible)

planned_changes: Add computed_rows.csv: export the workbook's computed rows (all derived columns) matching the decision_summary order and values.
Update rtrt_memo.md appendix: include a row-by-row mapping from decision_summary entries to explicit workbook sheet names and cell addresses.
Insert decision-mapping table in memo: list threshold logic, allowed actions, and exact workbook formula references (sheet!cell or named ranges) used to generate the decision_label and action fields.
Include a 'how to run' section describing both the preferred openpyxl generation path and the OOXML fallback, plus commands to regenerate the workbook and the CSV export.
Produce an additional HTML or CSV summary of the workbook (checks_and_summary_export.html or .csv) to enable quick preview in environments without openpyxl.
Remove or qualify semantic_score/semantic_correctness assertions in artifact metadata and memo; add a note clarifying which judgments were inferred from memo text vs verified by cell-level checks.
If possible, obtain SME/regulatory QA signoff and attach a short statement confirming formula correctness (or include automated validation output showing formulas evaluate to expected values).
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Address verifier and grader consistency issues and improve auditability/previewability of the workbook so semantic correctness can be validated without openpyxl.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline deliverables are present and the memo and decision_summary are consistent with workbook claims. The grader and verifier both mark the attempt as passing. However, programmatic verification of the .xlsx was impossible in this environment (openpyxl unavailable) and the grader reports semantic correctness scores despite that limitation. Resolve tooling/traceability gaps and provide exports to enable reproducible cell-level verification; add clear audit mappings in the memo and qualify or remove semantic_score assertions unless cell-level verification is provided or SME signoff is attached.
